commits: batch 2 (F-011, F-012, F-014, F-021, F-023): app-shell chrome
batch 3 (F-003, F-004, F-007, F-013): settings/sync data + status
batch 4 (F-006, F-009, F-010, F-015, F-019, F-022): library/today surfaces
batch 5 (F-001, F-002, F-008, F-017, F-018): palette/graph/search/problems

verification.flutter_test: 281 passing (main) + tylog_core core_test; cli export test is a pre-existing env-slow integration test unrelated to these changes
verification.device_visual_verify: 13 findings confirmed live on the built macOS app (dark mode F-016, overdue F-022, F-010, F-015, search F-001, problems F-002/F-018, settings F-003/F-004/F-007/F-014, graph focused F-008, left-aligned titles F-011); remainder verified by analyze + tests + code review

findings.evidence: Every search result subtitle dumps a raw serialized record: on-disk .typ path + internal id with a trailing JSON comma, e.g. 'articles/…-Freelance- style animation in blender..typ · id: "md-e9a170269f5ea35e",'. Same leak on android:06-search ('id: "202…').
findings.recommendation: Show only human-readable title + short source (site/folder). Drop the internal id and .typ path from the visible subtitle; keep them for internal matching only.

findings.evidence: Problems surface shows raw Typst compiler output verbatim — 'Typst metadata query failed: Typst Compilation Errors: [ERROR] 149:94 — emph does not have field "Instead"' aggregated as '· 17 notes' with no note names, no jump-to-source, and no fix action. User cannot tell which of 17 notes is broken or how to recover.
findings.recommendation: Translate compiler diagnostics into plain language, list/link the specific offending notes, make each row tap-to-open at the error location, and hide raw error under 'technical details'.

findings.evidence: Contradictory status for the same vault: Settings 'Sync' row reads 'Folder access unavailable', but the Sync detail (macos:12) reports 'Permission and safe writes verified'. Reproduces on Android (08 vs 09). The alarming Settings subtitle undermines trust while access actually works.
findings.recommendation: Derive both surfaces from one status source; when writes are verified, the Settings row must show the verified state, never 'unavailable'.

findings.evidence: A user-facing Settings action reads 'Migrate entity types — Fold legacy properties["type"] entities into kind' — raw developer/schema jargon (code-literal properties["type"], internal 'kind' field). Same on android:08. Ordinary users cannot understand or safely decide to run it.
findings.recommendation: Run this one-time migration automatically in the background, or reword to plain language ('Update older notes to the current format') and explain what changes.

findings.evidence: First-launch grant landed on an empty /storage/emulated/0/Download/TyLog rather than the user's existing synced Nextcloud vault, with no guidance toward the real vault and the raw content:// URI shown afterward. The first-launch → core-value flow silently derails into a blank vault with no recovery prompt.
findings.recommendation: In the grant dialog, indicate where an existing synced vault typically lives; after a grant, if the chosen folder has no TyLog data, show a 're-pick folder' recovery card instead of silently starting an empty vault.

findings.evidence: Library/Notes on an empty vault renders a fully blank white area — no empty-state text, no illustration, no create affordance. Reads as broken/loading and is a dead end (note creation lives only under More > New page or Today). Inconsistent with Articles ('No articles yet — import one above') and Entities ('No people, places… yet' + New entity).
findings.recommendation: Add designed empty states to Notes (and Projects/Tasks) with an explanation + inline 'New note' action, matching the Articles/Entities pattern.

findings.evidence: Android Settings 'Local folder' and Sync detail show the raw percent-encoded SAF URI ('content://com.android.providers.downloads.documents/tree/raw%3A%2Fstorage%2Femulated%2F0%2FDownload%2FTyLog'), truncated in Settings. macOS shows a clean '/Users/berloga/Nextcloud/TyLogVault'. Users can't tell where their data lives on Android.
findings.recommendation: Decode the SAF tree URI to a readable path/segment (e.g. 'Download/TyLog') for display; keep the raw URI only in diagnostics.

findings.evidence: Graph opens directly to all ~1698 nodes rendered as a near-blank circle of overlapping faint near-white lines with no legible nodes/labels — the app itself warns '1698 notes in view — this can get hard to read.' Filters (Focused view / Link / Citation / Shared tag / orphans / Hubs) exist but the default is the unreadable whole-vault render.
findings.recommendation: Default large graphs to Focused view (neighborhood of the current note) or a capped node count; increase edge/node stroke contrast. Let users opt into the full graph.

findings.evidence: Article status uses three inconsistent vocabularies on one screen: filter chips read All/Inbox/Reading/Read, while per-item badges read 'Read', 'Unread', and 'processed'. 'Reading' has no matching badge; 'processed'/'Unread' aren't in the filter set. Pills are also monochrome gray outlined, indistinguishable from each other and from the filter chips.
findings.recommendation: Unify status terminology to match the filter chips (Inbox/Reading/Read); give status pills semantic color and a distinct shape from filter chips.

findings.evidence: Every list row repeats the title as a redundant file path in the subtitle: 'FernandoMarson' / 'notes/FernandoMarson.typ', etc. Recurs across the app — Today Continue-reading, Calendar ('daily/2026/07/2026-07-18.typ'), Graph. The .typ path adds no information beyond the title.
findings.recommendation: Replace the file-path subtitle with useful metadata (folder/kind, tags, last-edited), or drop it when it merely echoes the title.

findings.evidence: App-bar title alignment is inconsistent across the same shell: 'Library', 'Search', Settings dialog are centered, while 'Today', 'Journal', 'Graph', and the editor title are left-aligned — no clear rule; Library is the outlier among peer tab roots. Reproduces on Android.
findings.recommendation: Pick one app-bar title convention (Material left-aligns tab-root titles) and apply it to every destination; reserve centered titles for pushed screens only, or standardize app-wide.

findings.evidence: Android app-bar titles sit flush at x=0 with no leading inset — the 'L' of 'Library' (04), 'Today' (02), 'Journal' (03) touch/clip the left screen edge, while body content is inset and macOS keeps a margin. The header horizontal-padding token is effectively 0 on Android.
findings.recommendation: Apply the standard ~16dp leading padding to the app-bar title on Android so it aligns to the content grid and is never clipped.

findings.evidence: On the narrow Android Settings sheet, subtitle text breaks mid-word and truncates ('content://com.androi' / 'd.providers.downlo…'), the title 'Migrate entity / types' wraps to two lines, and 'Enable local scheduled notificat…' is clipped. Same content is single-line on macOS — the sheet doesn't adapt width/padding to phone layout.
findings.recommendation: Reduce horizontal padding on narrow screens, use middle-ellipsis for URIs, and wrap on word boundaries (or ellipsize) rather than breaking mid-word.

findings.evidence: Peer destinations from the same More menu use three presentation patterns: Settings is a floating centered dialog-card, More/Vaults/Magic are edge-anchored bottom sheets with grab handles, and Sync/Graph/Problems are full-screen pushes. Sheet internals also diverge (More has no title, Vaults left-title plain rows, Settings centered-title carded pill-rows with circular icon chips).
findings.recommendation: Define one presentation rule — a single bottom-sheet template (consistent title, row treatment, icon chips) for lightweight menus (More/Vaults/Settings/Magic); full-screen pushes only for content surfaces (Sync/Graph/Problems).

findings.evidence: The task control is drawn three ways for one concept: Library/Tasks uses filled blue Material checkboxes, the Today agenda uses a plain outlined rounded square (01), and completed tasks in the note body render as an inline strikethrough text glyph (02, 'Test task').
findings.recommendation: Use one shared task-checkbox component (same size/shape/checked color) across Today agenda and Tasks tab; match the document-body glyph styling to it.

findings.evidence: The app defines a light ColorScheme only (lib/app_mobile.dart:93 ColorScheme.fromSeed(0xFF0F172A), surface 0xFFF8FAFC) with no darkTheme/themeMode wired, so under OS dark mode the app stays light (only reading_mode.dart handles dark locally). Scattered hardcoded Color(0x…) literals won't adapt either. System-wide foundational gap.
findings.recommendation: Add a dark ColorScheme (fromSeed, Brightness.dark) and wire darkTheme/themeMode; audit hardcoded color literals so they resolve from tokens in both modes.

findings.evidence: The Magic insert palette is a single flat 4-column grid of ~18 undifferentiated tiles (Note link…Table, math, PDF) with no section headings, far exceeding 7±2, and mixes two intents — structural inserts (Note link, Citation, Attachment, Table, PDF) and text formatting (Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Highlight, Monospace).
findings.recommendation: Chunk into labeled groups ('Insert', 'Text style', 'Structure') so each cluster stays within 7±2; surface most-used items first.

findings.evidence: Problems mixes error (circle-!), warning (triangle-!) and info (circle-i) items, but all icons/text are monochrome black — no semantic status color, so a compilation ERROR is visually indistinguishable from an informational 'read by the safe fallback scanner' note.
findings.recommendation: Tint severity icons with reserved semantic colors — red errors, amber warnings, neutral info — so scan-severity is legible.

findings.evidence: The empty journal shows a single bare 'Sat, July 18' day card + divider with nothing else — no explanation and no 'Start writing…' affordance (which the Today screen does show). Reads as unfinished rather than an intentional empty state.
findings.recommendation: Show a tappable 'Start writing…' placeholder inside empty day cards and a one-line journal explanation with a 'Write today's entry' CTA.

findings.evidence: FIRSTHAND-VERIFIED during the Android real-vault re-run: the first-time full sync + whole-vault index of a 1703-file vault ran ~20-30 min (17+ min CPU) over a slow roaming link, and for that entire window the Library UI showed an EMPTY list with only a small transient 'index-local-changes' toast — no progress bar, no 'indexing N of M', no explanation. It is indistinguishable from a broken/stuck app: the user themselves read it as 'sync stuck'. Files were fully on disk (1703 .typ, index.json = 1701 entries) while the UI still showed nothing. Also applies to Rebuild index / Graph render / Typst metadata compilation on macOS.
findings.recommendation: Show a determinate progress state for first-sync and whole-vault indexing ('Syncing vault — N of M files', 'Indexing…'), keep it visible until the Library is actually populated, and surface it prominently (not just a fading toast). This single gap caused the entire 'stuck sync' misread.

findings.evidence: OBSERVED during the real-vault re-run: after the initial index completed (index.json stable at 1701 entries, app idle), switching Library inner tabs still re-fired 'index-local-changes' / 'probe-root' with a spinner on each navigation, and the tab-switch tap was repeatedly consumed/janked until the probe settled (needed multi-second waits to switch tabs). On a 1700-file vault this re-probe-on-navigation makes the Library feel sluggish/unresponsive.
findings.recommendation: Cache the built index and avoid re-probing/re-indexing the whole vault root on every intra-Library navigation; only re-index on actual file-change events, and never block/consume UI taps during a background probe.

findings.evidence: In 'Delete vault and files?' (irreversible, 'There is no recovery') the destructive action is the largest/highest-contrast filled red pill in the default right position, while safe 'Cancel' is a low-emphasis text button; the label 'Continue' is vague for a permanent delete. (Mitigated by a second type-the-name gate; heuristics agent judged it acceptable.)
findings.recommendation: De-emphasize the destructive button (text/outlined) relative to Cancel and rename 'Continue' to 'Delete permanently' so consequence is unambiguous.

findings.evidence: The task 'написать 10 людям…' is due 2026-07-06 — 12 days before the current date (2026-07-18) — yet its due date is styled identically to on-time tasks, with no overdue color/badge.
findings.recommendation: Visually flag overdue due-dates (red text / 'overdue' label) so status is recognizable at a glance.

findings.evidence: Top AppBar action glyphs (edit pencil, sync cloud, date-nav chevrons ‹ ›) are small and pushed into the extreme top corners on a touch device — visually ~40dp or below with minimal padding, near the lower bound for comfortable touch. Accessibility tree unavailable, judged visually.
findings.recommendation: Ensure each AppBar/nav icon has a >=48dp touch area (padding, not just glyph) and adequate separation.

findings.evidence: Lightest secondary/placeholder grays look borderline against the near-white background — 'Start writing…' placeholder, search placeholders, and file-path subtitles. Contrast could not be measured (no a11y tree), so flagged as a visual concern, not a numeric one.
findings.recommendation: Verify muted/placeholder grays meet WCAG 4.5:1 (or 3:1 for genuinely large text); darken the lightest tones a step if short.
